name: PocketBase batch behavior
pocketBaseNote: PocketBase batch requests execute supported record mutations as a single transaction, inherit the parent request auth context, and reject unsupported child requests or batch setting violations.

cases.name: empty batch returns an empty response list
cases.pocketBaseNote: An empty batch is valid and returns an empty response array.

cases.name: successful batch commits all child record mutations
cases.pocketBaseNote: Successful child create, update, and delete record requests commit together and return one response entry per child request.

cases.name: parent auth context is forwarded to child requests
cases.pocketBaseNote: Child requests evaluate record rules with the auth context from the parent batch request.

cases.name: upsert batch creates a missing record
cases.pocketBaseNote: A PUT child request with a new id behaves like a record create.

cases.name: upsert batch updates an existing record
cases.pocketBaseNote: A PUT child request with an existing id behaves like a record update.

cases.name: failed child rolls back earlier child mutations
cases.pocketBaseNote: If any child request fails, the whole batch fails and earlier successful mutations from the same batch are rolled back.

cases.name: unsupported child request fails the batch
cases.pocketBaseNote: Batch children are limited to supported record mutation routes; unsupported routes fail the transaction.

cases.name: custom child auth header fails the batch
cases.pocketBaseNote: Child requests cannot override auth headers because batch auth should come from the parent request.

cases.name: maxRequests setting rejects oversized batches
cases.pocketBaseNote: The persisted batch maxRequests setting caps the number of child requests before execution starts.

cases.name: disabled batch setting rejects requests
cases.pocketBaseNote: Disabling the batch API in settings rejects new batch requests.
